children.heading: Petition to District Court
children.label: i Petition to District Court
children.text: Subsequent to a determination by the Secretary under that a financial company satisfies the criteria in , the Secretary shall notify the Corporation and the covered financial company. If the board of directors (or body performing similar functions) of the covered financial company acquiesces or consents to the appointment of the Corporation as receiver, the Secretary shall appoint the Corporation as receiver. If the board of directors (or body performing similar functions) of the covered financial company does not acquiesce or consent to the appointment of the Corporation as receiver, the Secretary shall petition the United States District Court for the District of Columbia for an order authorizing the Secretary to appoint the Corporation as receiver.section 5383 of this titlesection 5383(b) of this title

children.heading: Form and content of order
children.label: ii Form and content of order
children.text: The Secretary shall present all relevant findings and the recommendation made pursuant to to the Court. The petition shall be filed under seal.section 5383(a) of this title

children.heading: Determination
children.label: iii Determination
children.text: On a strictly confidential basis, and without any prior public disclosure, the Court, after notice to the covered financial company and a hearing in which the covered financial company may oppose the petition, shall determine whether the determination of the Secretary that the covered financial company is in default or in danger of default and satisfies the definition of a financial company under is arbitrary and capricious.section 5381(a)(11) of this title

children.label: I
children.text: is not arbitrary and capricious, the Court shall issue an order immediately authorizing the Secretary to appoint the Corporation as receiver of the covered financial company; or

children.label: II
children.text: is arbitrary and capricious, the Court shall immediately provide to the Secretary a written statement of each reason supporting its determination, and afford the Secretary an immediate opportunity to amend and refile the petition under clause (i).

children.heading: Petition granted by operation of law
children.label: v Petition granted by operation of law
I
children.text: the petition shall be granted by operation of law;

children.label: II
children.text: the Secretary shall appoint the Corporation as receiver; and

children.label: III
children.text: liquidation under this subchapter shall automatically and without further notice or action be commenced and the Corporation may immediately take all actions authorized under this subchapter.

children.heading: Effect of determination
children.label: B Effect of determination
children.text: The determination of the Court under subparagraph (A) shall be final, and shall be subject to appeal only in accordance with paragraph (2). The decision shall not be subject to any stay or injunction pending appeal. Upon conclusion of its proceedings under subparagraph (A), the Court shall provide immediately for the record a written statement of each reason supporting the decision of the Court, and shall provide copies thereof to the Secretary and the covered financial company.

children.heading: Criminal penalties
children.label: C Criminal penalties
children.text: A person who recklessly discloses a determination of the Secretary under or a petition of the Secretary under subparagraph (A), or the pendency of court proceedings as provided for under subparagraph (A), shall be fined not more than $250,000, or imprisoned for not more than 5 years, or both.section 5383(b) of this title

children.heading: Appeal of decisions of the District Court
children.label: 2 Appeal of decisions of the District Court

children.heading: Appeal to Court of Appeals
children.label: A Appeal to Court of Appeals

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: Subject to clause (ii), the United States Court of Appeals for the District of Columbia Circuit shall have jurisdiction of an appeal of a final decision of the Court filed by the Secretary or a covered financial company, through its board of directors, notwithstanding , not later than 30 days after the date on which the decision of the Court is rendered or deemed rendered under this subsection.section 5390(a)(1)(A)(i) of this title

children.heading: Condition of jurisdiction
children.label: ii Condition of jurisdiction
children.text: The Court of Appeals shall have jurisdiction of an appeal by a covered financial company only if the covered financial company did not acquiesce or consent to the appointment of a receiver by the Secretary under paragraph (1)(A).

children.heading: Expedition
children.label: iii Expedition
children.text: The Court of Appeals shall consider any appeal under this subparagraph on an expedited basis.

children.heading: Scope of review
children.label: iv Scope of review
children.text: For an appeal taken under this subparagraph, review shall be limited to whether the determination of the Secretary that a covered financial company is in default or in danger of default and satisfies the definition of a financial company under is arbitrary and capricious.section 5381(a)(11) of this title

children.heading: Appeal to the Supreme Court
children.label: B Appeal to the Supreme Court

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: A petition for a writ of certiorari to review a decision of the Court of Appeals under subparagraph (A) may be filed by the Secretary or the covered financial company, through its board of directors, notwithstanding , with the Supreme Court of the United States, not later than 30 days after the date of the final decision of the Court of Appeals, and the Supreme Court shall have discretionary jurisdiction to review such decision.section 5390(a)(1)(A)(i) of this title

children.heading: Written statement
children.label: ii Written statement
children.text: In the event of a petition under clause (i), the Court of Appeals shall immediately provide for the record a written statement of each reason for its decision.

children.heading: Expedition
children.label: iii Expedition
children.text: The Supreme Court shall consider any petition under this subparagraph on an expedited basis.

children.heading: Scope of review
children.label: iv Scope of review
children.text: Review by the Supreme Court under this subparagraph shall be limited to whether the determination of the Secretary that the covered financial company is in default or in danger of default and satisfies the definition of a financial company under is arbitrary and capricious.section 5381(a)(11) of this title

children.heading: Establishment and transmittal of rules and procedures
children.label: b Establishment and transmittal of rules and procedures

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: Not later than 6 months after , the Court shall establish such rules and procedures as may be necessary to ensure the orderly conduct of proceedings, including rules and procedures to ensure that the 24-hour deadline is met and that the Secretary shall have an ongoing opportunity to amend and refile petitions under subsection (a)(1).July 21, 2010

children.label: A
children.text: the Committee on the Judiciary of the Senate;

children.label: B
children.text: the Committee on Banking, Housing, and Urban Affairs of the Senate;

children.label: C
children.text: the Committee on the Judiciary of the House of Representatives; and

children.label: D
children.text: the Committee on Financial Services of the House of Representatives.

children.heading: Provisions applicable to financial companies
children.label: c Provisions applicable to financial companies

children.heading: Bankruptcy Code
children.label: 1 Bankruptcy Code
children.text: Except as provided in this subsection, the provisions of the Bankruptcy Code and rules issued thereunder or otherwise applicable insolvency law, and not the provisions of this subchapter, shall apply to financial companies that are not covered financial companies for which the Corporation has been appointed as receiver.

children.heading: This subchapter
children.label: 2 This subchapter
children.text: The provisions of this subchapter shall exclusively apply to and govern all matters relating to covered financial companies for which the Corporation is appointed as receiver, and no provisions of the Bankruptcy Code or the rules issued thereunder shall apply in such cases, except as expressly provided in this subchapter.

children.heading: Time limit on receivership authority
children.label: d Time limit on receivership authority

children.heading: Baseline period
children.label: 1 Baseline period
children.text: Any appointment of the Corporation as receiver under this section shall terminate at the end of the 3-year period beginning on the date on which such appointment is made.

children.label: i
children.text: maximize the net present value return from the sale or other disposition of the assets of the covered financial company; or

children.label: ii
children.text: minimize the amount of loss realized upon the sale or other disposition of the assets of the covered financial company; and

children.label: B
children.text: to protect the stability of the financial system of the United States.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: The time limit under this subsection, as extended under paragraph (2), may be extended for up to 1 additional year, if the Chairperson of the Corporation, with the concurrence of the Secretary, submits the certifications described in paragraph (2).

children.heading: Additional report required
children.label: B Additional report required
children.text: Not later than 30 days after the date of commencement of the extension under subparagraph (A), the Corporation shall submit a report to the Committee on Banking, Housing, and Urban Affairs of the Senate and the Committee on Financial Services of the House of Representatives describing the need for the extension and the specific plan of the Corporation to conclude the receivership before the end of the second extension.

children.label: A
children.text: the Council determines that the Corporation used its best efforts to conclude the receivership in accordance with its plan before the end of the time limit described in paragraph (3);

children.label: B
children.text: the Council determines that the completion of longer-term responsibilities in the form of ongoing litigation justifies the need for an extension; and

children.label: i
children.text: the ongoing litigation justifying the need for an extension; and

children.label: ii
children.text: the specific plan of the Corporation to complete the litigation and conclude the receivership.

children.heading: Regulations
children.label: 5 Regulations
children.text: The Corporation may issue regulations governing the termination of receiverships under this subchapter.

children.heading: No liability
children.label: 6 No liability
children.text: The Corporation and the Deposit Insurance Fund shall not be liable for unresolved claims arising from the receivership after the termination of the receivership.

children.heading: Study of bankruptcy and orderly liquidation process for financial companies
children.label: e Study of bankruptcy and orderly liquidation process for financial companies

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: The Administrative Office of the United States Courts and the Comptroller General of the United States shall each monitor the activities of the Court, and each such Office shall conduct separate studies regarding the bankruptcy and orderly liquidation process for financial companies under the Bankruptcy Code.

children.label: i
children.text: the effectiveness of chapter 7 or chapter 11 of the Bankruptcy Code in facilitating the orderly liquidation or reorganization of financial companies;

children.label: ii
children.text: ways to maximize the efficiency and effectiveness of the Court; and

children.label: iii
children.text: ways to make the orderly liquidation process under the Bankruptcy Code for financial companies more effective.

children.heading: Reports
children.label: 2 Reports
children.text: Not later than 1 year after , in each successive year until the third year, and every fifth year after that date, the Administrative Office of the United States Courts and the Comptroller General of the United States shall submit to the Committee on Banking, Housing, and Urban Affairs and the Committee on the Judiciary of the Senate and the Committee on Financial Services and the Committee on the Judiciary of the House of Representatives separate reports summarizing the results of the studies conducted under paragraph (1).July 21, 2010

children.heading: Study of international coordination relating to bankruptcy process for financial companies
children.label: f Study of international coordination relating to bankruptcy process for financial companies

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: The Comptroller General of the United States shall conduct a study regarding international coordination relating to the orderly liquidation of financial companies under the Bankruptcy Code.

children.label: i
children.text: the extent to which international coordination currently exists;

children.label: ii
children.text: current mechanisms and structures for facilitating international cooperation;

children.label: iii
children.text: barriers to effective international coordination; and

children.label: iv
children.text: ways to increase and make more effective international coordination.

children.heading: Report
children.label: 2 Report
children.text: Not later than 1 year after , the Comptroller General of the United States shall submit to the Committee on Banking, Housing, and Urban Affairs and the Committee on the Judiciary of the Senate and the Committee on Financial Services and the Committee on the Judiciary of the House of Representatives and the Secretary a report summarizing the results of the study conducted under paragraph (1).July 21, 2010

children.heading: Study of prompt corrective action implementation by the appropriate Federal agencies
children.label: g Study of prompt corrective action implementation by the appropriate Federal agencies

children.heading: Study
children.label: 1 Study
children.text: The Comptroller General of the United States shall conduct a study regarding the implementation of prompt corrective action by the appropriate Federal banking agencies.

children.label: A
children.text: the effectiveness of implementation of prompt corrective action by the appropriate Federal banking agencies and the resolution of insured depository institutions by the Corporation; and

children.label: B
children.text: ways to make prompt corrective action a more effective tool to resolve the insured depository institutions at the least possible long-term cost to the Deposit Insurance Fund.

children.heading: Report to Council
children.label: 3 Report to Council
children.text: Not later than 1 year after , the Comptroller General shall submit a report to the Council on the results of the study conducted under this subsection.July 21, 2010

children.heading: Council report of action
children.label: 4 Council report of action
children.text: Not later than 6 months after the date of receipt of the report from the Comptroller General under paragraph (3), the Council shall submit a report to the Committee on Banking, Housing, and Urban Affairs of the Senate and the Committee on Financial Services of the House of Representatives on actions taken in response to the report, including any recommendations made to the Federal primary financial regulatory agencies under .section 5330 of this title
